title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scrapy Component Flow
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 5810 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scrapy Component Flow.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crawl Observability
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-f1i0rw" that uses case 2290 to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-f1i0rw
# Competency: scraper-crawl-observability.
# Case 2290: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_crawl_observability_f1i0rw(component_boundaries: int = 8, verified_boundaries: int = 8, middleware_orders: int = 6, metric_families: int = 10, hidden_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_f1i0rw = "f1i0rw-wr0i1f-2290"
    assert evidence_variant_f1i0rw
    evidence_axes_f1i0rw = (
        "authorizationf1i0rw",
        "url2290",
        "responsewr0i1f",
        "treef1i0rw2290",
        "frontier2290f1i0rw",
        "reportwr0i1f2290",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_f1i0rw)) == 6
    if component_boundaries < 6 or verified_boundaries < 0:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "scrapy-architecture"
    if verified_boundaries != component_boundaries:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "component-reconciliation"
    if middleware_orders < 4 or metric_families < 8:
        return False, metric_families, "scale-evidence"
    if hidden_failures > 0:
        return False, hidden_failures, "observability-gap"
    assert verified_boundaries == component_boundaries and hidden_failures == 0
    return True, metric_families, "observable-scrapy-transfer"
